comment: Candidate-template registry for extra/qk_candidate_template_gen.py. v0 is NARROW and explicit: each template expands into a SMALL, fixed set of legal decode candidate specs (in the search_candidates schema) plus intentionally-invalid variants that prove policy pruning. Templates generate SPECS, never kernel code or new flags; executable variants bind to EXISTING decode_eval candidates.

templates.description: Candidates on the known FLASH_L KV-split axis (the decode T=1 'manufacture parallel work from KV splits' lever).
templates.expansion_rule: one executable candidate per flash_l value (128->baseline_default, 64->flash_l_64) + one invalid promote_default variant for flash_l=64
closed_lane_guards: no bounded vector-tile tuning beyond the known FLASH_L axis
FLASH_L=64 non-promotable unless a future W==D clears the >=5%@1024 gate
templates.binding_strategy: bind to existing decode_eval candidate by flash_l: 128->baseline_default, 64->flash_l_64
templates.stop_condition: FLASH_L=64 stays non-promotable unless future W==D clears the gate; do not widen the sweep
templates.notes: small + fixed; no broad sweep. The promote variant proves promotion-intent pruning.

templates.description: q8 FFN handwritten route as an opt-in candidate, plus an invalid default-promotion variant.
templates.expansion_rule: one executable opt_in candidate (->q8_opt_in) + one invalid default_attempt (promote_default intent)
closed_lane_guards: q8 is opt-in only; default promotion forbidden without owner approval
templates.binding_strategy: opt_in -> existing decode_eval candidate q8_opt_in; default_attempt -> pruned before eval
templates.stop_condition: PASS_OPT_IN is bankable; a default flip requires an explicit owner decision
templates.notes: proves opt-in is allowed but default promotion is pruned by policy.

templates.description: Intentionally-invalid candidates that reopen closed decode lanes; all must be pruned before any benchmark.
templates.expansion_rule: one non-executable candidate per closed lane, tagged with closed_lane_risk
templates.binding_strategy: no decode_eval binding; pruned on closed_lane_risk
templates.stop_condition: these lanes stay closed; never benchmark
templates.notes: proves the generator emits policy metadata and the loop refuses closed lanes before eval.

templates.description: The remaining north-star lever: a full llama-style vector flash_attn_tile with efficient many-KV-split / stream-k combine. NOT runnable yet (no evaluator binding / kernel exists).
templates.expansion_rule: one non-executable, deferred candidate with no decode_eval binding
closed_lane_guards: not a closed lane; legal future work, but no kernel/evaluator binding exists
templates.binding_strategy: no decode_eval binding; deferred -> PRUNE_NEEDS_TEMPLATE (needs an evaluator-binding template)
templates.stop_condition: deferred until a flash_attn_tile kernel + evaluator binding exists; never pretend it is runnable
templates.notes: shows the generator can represent future work honestly without benchmarking it.
